claimText: Tardigrades are eight-legged segmented micro-animals commonly known as Water Bears or Moss Piglets.
evidenceExcerpt: Also known as Water Bears or Moss Piglets, these half-millimetre long, eight-legged segmented micro-animals

claimText: Tardigrades endure extreme temperatures, pressures, and radiation through cryptobiosis, which is a state of extreme inactivity.
evidenceExcerpt: endure extreme temperatures, crushing pressures, and radiation through cryptobiosis. Cryptobiosis is a state of extreme inactivity in response to adverse environmental conditions.
examUse: Concept definition or statement-based question on survival mechanisms.

claimText: Recognized classes of Tardigrades include Eutardigrada and Heterotardigrada.
evidenceExcerpt: Recognized classes include Eutardigrada and Heterotardigrada.

claimText: Tardigrades are being taken to the International Space Station (ISS) for experiments by Indian astronaut Shubhanshu Shukla.
evidenceExcerpt: Indian astronaut Shubhanshu Shukla will take Tardigrades to the International Space Station (ISS) for an experiment.
distractorHooks: Taken to China's Tiangong space station
